channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden is making quick trip to Berlin before US election to discuss Ukraine and democracy with allies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has long believed diplomacy is about personal relationships — and he'll spend Friday in Berlin with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz as his time in office is on the cusp of ending.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to headline sold-out Al Smith charity dinner as Harris sends pre-recorded video message
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the Al Smith Catholic charity dinner in New York on Thursday, after VP Harris snubbed the event and will send a video message instead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois alderwoman claims she was arrested after criticizing the mayor
item.description: Alderwoman Colby Chapman said she spent five hours in jail, in what she calls a politically motivated abuse of power.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Majority of Pennsylvania county sheriffs 'wholeheartedly endorse' Trump for president
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: 49 of 67 county sheriffs in battleground state Pennsylvania endorsed former President Trump on Thursday, saying they are “confident in his leadership and ability to safeguard our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris says the killing of Sinwar is an opportunity 'to finally end the war in Gaza'
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris said Thursday the killing of Hamas' top leader, Yahya Sinwar, by Israel “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early voting begins in North Carolina, officials helping after Helene
item.description: With the election less than three weeks away, many residents are still reeling from Helene. This has led to some drastic changes to the voting process.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: Nebraska high court restores voting right for thousands of convicted felons
item.description: The court's unanimous decision restores the right to vote for residents with felony criminal records upon completion of their sentence, impacting thousands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: McConnell also criticized the former president as a “narcissist” and admitted after the 2020 election that “it’s not just the Democrats who are counting the days” until he left office.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: GOP cringes over Trump’s vow to wield military against opponents
item.description: Former President Trump’s incendiary comments about deploying the military and using other powers of the executive branch to pursue his political enemies, including a sitting member of Congress, is putting downballot Republican candidates on the defensive.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: VP Harris' 'Agenda for Black men' not as exclusive as advertised
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris' presidential campaign says that her proposed "Opportunity Agenda for Black Men" will be open to individuals of all races and genders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-funeral home owner pleads guilty to assaulting police and journalists during Capitol riot
item.description: A former Long Island funeral home owner has pleaded guilty to spraying wasp killer at police officers and assaulting two journalists, including an Associated Press photographer, during a mob’s riot at the U.S. Capitol nearly four years ago.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Comer report reveals Biden-Harris admin's 'rampant waste, fraud, abuse'
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer, R-Ky., highlighted his panel's oversight work of the Biden-Harris administration, saying Americans “cannot afford" another term of “incompetence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey battleground House district poll finds tight race as GOP seeks to retain seat
item.description: GOP incumbent Tom Kean and Democrat challenger Sue Altman battle are locked in a tight race for New Jersey’s 7th Congressional District, a new poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panel looking into Trump assassination attempt says Secret Service needs ‘fundamental reform’
item.description: An independent panel investigating the attempted assassination of Donald Trump at a Pennsylvania campaign rally says the Secret Service needs fundamental reform” and new leadership that “another Butler can and will happen again” without major changes in how candidates are protected.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: FLASHBACK: Reaction to Harris' plagiarism story much more benign than for Biden during '88 election
item.description: Reactions to plagiarism accusations levied against Kamala Harris seem more benign than what Joe Biden faced in his presidential bid in 1988 after being accused of the same thing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s UN ambassador: Response to Iran will be ‘very painful'
item.description: The world awaits Israel’s response to Iran’s missile attacks on Oct. 1, and the nation’s U.N. ambassador, Danny Danon, promised it will be "very painful" in order to deter Iran from attacking again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Epic clash: Pressed by Fox News, Kamala Harris comes out swinging
item.description: In her first interview on Fox News, Kamala Harris ducked questions, raised her voice and appeared to be trying to project an image of standing up to both Trump and the network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris calls on Republican voters to put 'country first' as Trump woos Latino voters
item.description: WASHINGTON CROSSING, Pa. (AP) — Surrounded by more than 100 former Republican officeholders and officials, Democrat Kamala Harris urged GOP voters on Wednesday to put “country first” and abandon Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris interview with Fox News showcases a change in strategy for Democrats with network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Kamala Harris ' interview with Fox News Channel's Bret Baier on Wednesday is the latest indication that Democrats during this campaign are increasingly willing to engage with a network well-stocked with supporters of opponent Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump would be the oldest person to become president. He’s not sharing health details
item.description: If he wins next month’s election, Donald Trump would be the oldest person in U.S. history elected president. Yet the 78-year-old Republican nominee is refusing to disclose new details about his physical or mental well-being, breaking decades of precedent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Liz Cheney jumps into House races — for Democrats
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) is taking her anti-Trump crusade to the congressional battlefield, endorsing Democrats in a pair of contested House races in a sign that she sees the lower chamber as a potential check on former President Trump if he wins a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane fallout threatens to hinder voting
item.description: The fallout from a pair of deadly hurricanes threatens to roil voting in the Southeast with complications for election officials and residents alike in states key to this year’s presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: In close Minn. House races, personal problems old and new can be vexing for candidates
item.description: With the Minnesota House majority hinging on a small number of seats, flaws in candidate pasts are resurfacing as parties and groups jockey for an edge that can decide key races.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Harris' Fox interview a bigger risk for Bret Baier: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses the state of the race as Kamala Harris and Donald Trump work to energize key voter groups.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Hennepin County achieves 'functional zero' status in ending veteran homelessness
item.description: The designation from several federal agencies indicates a community has brought down the number of veterans experiencing homelessness to a very low level and continues to sustain the effort.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Voting rights groups seek investigation into Wisconsin text message
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Voting rights advocates on Tuesday asked state and federal authorities to investigate anonymous text messages apparently targeting young Wisconsin voters, warning them not to vote in a state where they are ineligible.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: One-quarter of applicants ruled out colleges because of states' political climate: Poll
item.description: Out of that group, about two-thirds decided to exclude certain schools at the beginning of their search, the organization found.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Jean-Pierre blocking Kirby from White House podium, report says
item.description: Karine Jean-Pierre leads the daily press briefing, while John Kirby often handles questions about foreign affairs.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Giant pandas will arrive at D.C.'s National Zoo this week
item.description: After an 11-month wait, giant pandas will populate the zoo once again. A pair of pandas, which left China on FedEx's "Panda Express" cargo jet, are expected to arrive in the D.C. area on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC
